title: Fix Windows compilation: add signgam compat header for core-math lgamma.c

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Plan: Add a build.rs to ferray-ufunc that detects Windows target and injects a compat header declaring signgam via CFLAGS/DEP_CORE_MATH_* env. Also add the compat header file itself. This works around core-math-sys vendored lgamma.c using POSIX-only signgam global. Ref: GitHub issue dollspace-gay/ferray#5

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: core-math-sys already ships lib/signgam.c with 'int signgam;' and compiles it into the static lib. The issue is that lgamma.c does '#include <math.h> // for signgam' which declares signgam on POSIX but not on Windows. The fix is to declare signgam as extern in a compat header and inject it via CFLAGS. But since core-math-sys is an upstream dep with its own build.rs, we can't modify its compilation. Instead, we need to add the compat header at the ferray-ufunc level using a build.rs that sets the CFLAGS env var before core-math-sys compiles.

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Actually, CFLAGS set in a downstream build.rs won't affect an upstream -sys crate's cc::Build. The cc crate reads CFLAGS from the environment at cargo invocation time, not from DEP_ vars. The correct approach: set CFLAGS='-include signgam_compat.h' as a cargo config env var for Windows targets. We'll create a .cargo/config.toml entry and document the workaround, plus create the compat header.

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Revised approach: The cc crate reads CFLAGS from the process environment. A downstream build.rs cannot inject env vars into upstream -sys crate builds. The correct fix is: (1) Create a compat header at a known project path, (2) Add .cargo/config.toml with [env] CFLAGS for Windows targets pointing to that header, (3) Document for CI. The header declares 'extern int signgam;' only on _WIN32.

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: After further analysis: build.rs env vars don't propagate to upstream -sys crate builds. The cc crate reads CFLAGS from the process env at build time. Best approach: create .cargo/config.toml with [target.'cfg(windows)'.env] CFLAGS setting. But cargo's [env] table doesn't support per-target filtering. So instead: use a build.rs in ferray-ufunc that writes the header to OUT_DIR and prints cargo:rerun-if-changed, plus document that Windows users need to set CFLAGS. Actually simplest: use cargo's [env] unconditionally — the header has #if _WIN32 guards so it's a no-op on other platforms.

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: cargo config.toml [env] with relative=true prepends the workspace path to the ENTIRE value, not just the file path portion. This turns '-include compat/signgam_compat.h' into '/abs/path/-include compat/signgam_compat.h' which cc interprets wrong. Need a different approach.

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Solution: CPATH with relative=true resolves to absolute workspace path for the compat/ include dir. CFLAGS=-include signgam_compat.h force-includes the header. The cc crate picks up both env vars when building core-math-sys. Header has #if _WIN32 guard so it's a no-op on POSIX. All 48 test suites pass on Linux.
